feed.title: SAP Community - SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central
feed.subtitle: SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Permission Roles/Groups - Restrict access to entities to self
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm using the SAP SuccessFactors OData API to retrieve certain entities via a dedicated API user. However, I’m trying to figure out whether it’s possible to configure permission roles or groups in a way that lets me retrieve only the entities that are relevant to the end user (not the API user), based on specific business rules or dynamic criteria.

For example, take the Cost Center Foundation Object: I want to allow certain users to query cost centers, but only the ones where they are assigned as the cost center manager.

I know that in Business Rules, there is a way to check if a user is the currently logged-in user—like in this screenshot:

But I couldn't find any documentation or approach that allows something similar at the permission level. I'm not even sure if it's possible, but maybe someone has encountered a similar requirement and solved it with a different workaround (e.g. via MDF roles, custom filters, or proxy logic).

Any insight or experience would be greatly appreciated!

Best regards,

entry.title: Updated HRIS Mappings - but HRIS Sync job not updating the users data
entry.content:
Hi all,

Currently using an implementation test tenant, that has over 1200 default users provided by SAP.

I have added some HRIS mappings, so that Custom1, Custom2 should be updated with different data. (e.g. setting placing Standard Weekly Hours into Custom12. See below

However, when I run the HRIS Sync job, the data is not updating for all the users. I would have expected that Custom12 would now be updated with users Standard Hours.

The only way it did eventually sync the mappings was to make a change directly to the user. So I went and changed Standard Hours from 40 to 39. This had a knock on effect, and now for only that user - the custom fields have mapped correctly.

How do I get all users to have their Custom Fields updated?

Thanks,

Ed

entry.title: How to Auto-Populate Manager Name in Department Based on Custom Position Field in Successfactors?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have a requirement in SAP SuccessFactors where I added two custom fields in the Department (FO_Department) object:

  1. cust_chiefPos – a custom field of type Position (Foundation Object), used to store the manager position of the department.

  2. cust_chiefName – intended to store the name of the user who holds that position.

The goal is:
Whenever a user selects a manager position (cust_chiefPos) in the department, I want the cust_chiefName field to be automatically filled with the name of the user (incumbent) who holds that position.

I encountered issues accessing userNav or the incumbent details in the rule editor.
Also, I'm unsure whether cust_chiefName should be a String, or if it's possible to use User type directly and still auto-populate it.

Any help or best practice is highly appreciated!
Thanks in advance 🙏

entry.title: Termination - Time account days over
entry.content:
Hello experts,

The payout profile feature in a termination allows you to create a spot bonus with a partial or total amount of days (recalculated with the termination rule). However, if you have used more vacation days than the recalculation calculates, the system doesn't create a deduction. 

For example:

I accrued 30 days on 01/01/2025, I use 15, and on 02/01/2025 they terminate me. The recalculation says that I owe X days to the company. I'm wondering if using the purchase payout profile I can create a deduction like payout profile can do it. I think that is not possible, and the easiest workaround is that the HR create a deduction of X days in the employee profile or use some kind of integration between ISC-IC. If someone knows a better option, I would be glad to hear it.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Performance Objectives - Story reporting
entry.content:
Dear all, 

The question involves elements of both RBP and Story reporting. A performance objective is an agreement between the employee and their manager. However, our Center of Excellence (CoE) wants to monitor whether each employee has set their objectives. Using RBP, we can access the objective form, but this opens the content of the objectives. In the story report, I exclude the descriptions and focus only on the high-level counts (Objectives ID/Status). 

Can we report only on the objective IDs and statuses, without exposing the full objective content? 

Thanks

Mirjam

entry.title: Error message when trying to create 2 new users
entry.content:
Hello experts,

This error message appears when we try to import 2 users at the same time: a manager is hired after his direct report:

entry.title: EDIT HIRE RECORD BY IMPORT
entry.content:
Hello experts,

please is there a way to edit new hires records by import?

entry.title: FieldGlass integration for requisition creation
entry.content:
For a customer we are trying to connect SAP Fieldglass with SAP SuccessFactors. We are running into the issue that on the one hand SAP Fieldglass needs to use SAP Identity Access Services (IAS) as the identity provider to be able to login (https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/configuration-sap-fieldglass-sso-with-sap-cloud-identity-services-identity/ba-p/13747208) and on the other hand SAP SuccessFactors needs to be setup as the identity provider to be able to create requisitions from positions (https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-employee-central/using-sap-fieldglass-with-employee-central/using-saml-single-sign-on-sso).

We are not really getting anywhere with SAP support as we are running in circles. In SuccessFactors Provisioning it shows that the integration with Fieldglass is not supported.

SAP Fieldglass only support the configuration of a single identity provider.

This means we are left with the choice to either be able to login to Fieldglass or be able to create requisitions.

Has anyone gotten the integration between SuccessFactors and Fieldglass to work properly?

Some additional documentation:

  • https://me.sap.com/notes/3468737/E
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_FIELDGLASS_INTEGRATION/31c37f12fb734bd5a3b6039108a9c3ad/43347255fcb745aaa4f52764441fa50b.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Error Occurred while opening Compensation Info Portlet in Employee Profile
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Post release updates of 1H 2025 in test systems we are unable to open compensation information portlet in Employee Central, employee profile. We are coming across below error and couldn't find any relevant KB document for the same. 

Any suggestions/inputs on this regard would be highly appreciable.

Regards,

Prasanthi Takkellapati

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central SAP SuccessFactors Compensation

entry.title: how to set Granted user's company as a target population
entry.content:
Hello experts,

Please I have 10 administrators, each administrator should manage employees of his own company, should I create 10 roles for each administrator to define target population, or there is better solution?

entry.title: Fetching deleted records in Integration center
entry.content:
Hi Everyone, 

Hope you all doing great.

I'm currently wondering if there is possibility to fetch deleted records in Integration center?

In my case, I use IC (integration center) to store all relationship type from EC Dependent portlet to custom mdf.

this mdf is used for validating benefit reimbursement eligibility for employee's dependent, because currently fetching dependent data for Benefit Reimbursement is not possible. (actually possible, but SAP recommends using lookup to mdf object due to heavy employee count for our client).

However, I feel like there is potential issue where if one record is deleted in EC Dependent portlet, IC won't possible to fetch this deleted record. (delete access is granted to ESS role).

So, any feedback how to solve issue like this?

Thanks,

Reza

entry.title: reference a custom field in business rule
entry.content:
Hi,

I need to create a business rule that filters the accessibility through the use of one of the custom fields. But it is hard to find this field under the "Context" tree. I have set the custom field "custom01" using the "Manage Business Configuration" and then under "Employee Profile" I chose "standard" to reach for the custom field configuration. Any clue on how/where to find that custom field?

entry.title: oninit rule not triggered after employee import
entry.content:
Hello community,

We've configured to autopoulate the value "5" for "working days per week' field, this oninit rule successfully works in add new employee, but the value does not autopopulate using import employee data

entry.title: A new field is required Children Claimed for Tax which is only req for certain rel-types
entry.content:
Hello,

 

Hope you are doing well!!

We have a requirement to create a custom Field in Dependent information for Germany Only.

 

We have created the field in Global Information for Germany which is only enabled for Dependents.

Continuing with this we have to maintain a condition for Relationship type, it should display only when Relationship type is Child or Child with Disability.

 

We have tried creating multiple rules but nothing works. We have assigned the rule to Onchange scenario on Relationship type field, also tried cross entity rules.

 

Could anyone please help us with the setup?

Many thanks in advance.

entry.title: Real-Time Configuration Progress Tracker in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
While exploring SAP Learning Hub, I noticed a powerful yet simple UX feature — real-time tracking of progress. It shows learners how many topics they’ve completed, how many are left, and even displays a clean progress bar. This got me thinking…

Why don’t we have something similar inside SAP SuccessFactors?

As consultants, partners, and administrators, we often work across modules like:

  • Compensation

  • Performance and Goals (PMGM)

  • Employee Central (EC)

  • Onboarding, and more…

But there’s no built-in progress tracker to visually reflect how much of the configuration or project setup is completed during implementation or maintenance. Imagine if we could see a simple status like:

6 of 10 key configuration items completed
Progress: 60%
Pending: Eligibility rules, Route Map, EC Integration

if this could be done then it will reduce multiple clicks and improve SAP ecosystem

Benefits of a Real-Time Configuration Progress Tracker in SAP SuccessFactors

 Benefit Area  Description
Visibility & TransparencyClearly shows what’s configured vs. pending. No more scattered status updates.
Implementation EfficiencyKeeps consultants, admins, and PMs aligned. Faster go-lives, fewer blockers.
Customer ConfidenceClients can monitor progress visually in real time. Builds trust and clarity.
Agile EnablementSupports iterative delivery, sprint-based rollouts, and milestone tracking.
Error ReductionStandardized checklists prevent missing key setup steps.
Post-Go-Live UseUseful for ongoing maintenance, updates, and change requests.
Team CollaborationAll module owners see the same live status. Improves cross-functional flow.
GamificationAdd motivation through visual progress bars and milestone badges.
UX AlignmentMatches SAP Learning Hub’s intuitive learner UX. User-first for admins too.

 

Suggest me your ideas as well, i am new to the role and exploring SAP successfactor.

entry.title: What is difference between Forward Propagation and HRIS Sync in SuccessFactors?
entry.content: What is difference between Forward Propagation and HRIS Sync in SuccessFactors?

entry.title: Bank Account Details - Automated Cleanout
entry.content:
Hi community,

We would like to create a rule/job to clean out the information regarding Bank Account Details after 30 days from last effective date.

Have anyone managed to fix this or what do you recommend?

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Global Benefits HCM Employee Self-Service SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Service Center 

Kind regards,

entry.title: How to create a special leave balance to only a particular location employees?
entry.content:
Hi Community folks,

I would like to know, How to create a special leave balance to only a particular location employees?

entry.title: How do establish a connection between SuccessFactors Employee Central and S/4 HANA?
entry.content:
Hi Community Folks,

Could you please explain the process for establishing an integration between SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central and SAP S/4HANA?

I would appreciate a detailed overview, including any necessary components, tools, or configuration steps involved.

entry.title: Will Alumni count towards your license (seat) usage?
entry.content:
Hi,

Will Alumni count towards your SF license count?

Regards

Frans
